steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The question asks for the main purpose of the text. Read the passage for its central focus: it opens by announcing Saturn is the first planet discovered to have more than 100 moons, gives the count of newly detected moons, and ends with Saturn surpassing Jupiter. Every sentence serves that discovery.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• "To note a new finding about the number of Saturn's moons.": The passage opens with the discovery, supports it with specific evidence (62 new moons), and concludes with its significance (Saturn surpasses Jupiter). This captures the main purpose. ✓
  • "To explain how the CFHT works.": The CFHT is mentioned only as the instrument used — the passage never describes how it works. ✗
  • "To describe the atmospheric conditions of Jupiter.": Jupiter is mentioned only as a comparison point for moon count; its atmosphere is never discussed. ✗
  • "To discuss the history of Hawaii.": Hawaii is mentioned only as the location of the CFHT; the text has nothing to do with Hawaiian history. ✗
That matches the correct answer — A is correct.
